title: Dimapur Kalibari
tagline: Dimapur Kalibari — spiritual town of Nagaland

overview.short: Dimapur Kalibari is a temple of the Hindu goddess Kali in the town of Dimapur in Nagaland state of India.
overview.description: Dimapur Kalibari is a temple of the Hindu goddess Kali in the town of Dimapur in Nagaland state of India. It was built in 1956.

topPlaces.name: Dimapur
topPlaces.description: Dimapur () is the largest city and municipality in the Indian state of Nagaland. As of 2024 , the municipality had a population of 172,000.

topPlaces.name: Kuda, Dimapur
topPlaces.description: Kuda, formerly Nagarjan, is an Angami Naga village, located in the Dimapur District of the Indian state of Nagaland, India. Founded in 1941, Kuda is the oldest recognised Naga village in Dimapur area and one of the biggest in Nagaland.

topPlaces.name: Ruins of Kachari Rajbari
topPlaces.description: Ruins of Kachari Rajbari are a set of medieval monuments located in Dimapur, Nagaland, in Northeast India. Their history dates back to the 10th century when they appeared during the Kachari civilization.

topPlaces.name: Dimapur Jain Temple
topPlaces.description: Dimapur Jain Temple is mainly known for a Jain Temple in Dimapur which is built in 1947. The temple is located on Jain Temple Road and has Lord Mahavira as its Moolnayak.

topPlaces.name: Kirha
topPlaces.description: Kirha is a village located in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarters.

topPlaces.name: Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Chümoukedima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The sanctuary spans approximately 4.7 km² (470 hectares) and was notified as a wildlife sanctuary in 1986 to conserve remnant forest patches and native wildlife in the western Nagaland landscape.

topPlaces.name: Nagaland Zoological Park
topPlaces.description: The Nagaland Zoological Park is a 176-hectare (430-acre) zoo located at Rangapahar in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is Nagaland's biggest zoo and was opened to public in August 2008.

topPlaces.name: Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium or Sovima Cricket Stadium is a cricket stadium located at Sovima in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is the only cricket stadium in the northeastern Indian state of Nagaland.

items.note: Dimapur () is the largest city and municipality in the Indian state of Nagaland. As of 2024 , the municipality had a population of 172,000.

Kuda, formerly Nagarjan, is an Angami Naga village, located in the Dimapur District of the Indian state of Nagaland, India. Founded in 1941, Kuda is the oldest recognised Naga village in Dimapur area and one of the biggest in Nagaland.

Ruins of Kachari Rajbari are a set of medieval monuments located in Dimapur, Nagaland, in Northeast India. Their history dates back to the 10th century when they appeared during the Kachari civilization.
Dimapur Jain Temple is mainly known for a Jain Temple in Dimapur which is built in 1947. The temple is located on Jain Temple Road and has Lord Mahavira as its Moolnayak.
Kirha is a village located in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland and is a suburb of Chümoukedima, the district headquarters.

Rangapahar Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Chümoukedima District of the Indian state of Nagaland. The sanctuary spans approximately 4.7 km² (470 hectares) and was notified as a wildlife sanctuary in 1986 to conserve remnant forest patches and native wildlife in the western Nagaland landscape.
The Nagaland Zoological Park is a 176-hectare (430-acre) zoo located at Rangapahar in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is Nagaland's biggest zoo and was opened to public in August 2008.

The Nagaland Cricket Association Stadium or Sovima Cricket Stadium is a cricket stadium located at Sovima in the Chümoukedima District of Nagaland. It is the only cricket stadium in the northeastern Indian state of Nagaland.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dimapur Kalibari?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dimapur Kalibari is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dimapur Kalibari?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (1 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dimapur Kalibari?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dimapur Kalibari at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dimapur Kalibari?
faq.a: Stays in Dimapur Kalibari start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dimapur Kalibari?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dimapur, Kuda, Dimapur, Ruins of Kachari Rajbari, Dimapur Jain Temple, Kirha.
